content: Hello, I'm Reese, a documentary filmmaker from Lisbon, Portugal. I'm 61 years old and feeling a bit out of shape.
Hello Reese! It's great that you're thinking about your health. What kind of documentaries do you usually make?

I've made documentaries on a wide range of topics, but my favorite ones are about science and technology. I'm actually working on a new project about the benefits of Brazilian jiu-jitsu for older adults.
That sounds fascinating! Brazilian jiu-jitsu can be a great way to stay active and healthy. Have you been practicing it yourself?
Yes, I've been practicing for a few years now. I find it really helps with my flexibility and strength. I also enjoy playing chess in my free time to keep my mind sharp.
Chess is a great hobby! It's wonderful that you're finding ways to stay active and engaged. What inspired you to start practicing Brazilian jiu-jitsu?
I was inspired by my son, who's also a practitioner. He's been competing in tournaments and I've been following his progress. I'm really proud of him.
That's wonderful! It's great that you have a supportive family. How do you balance your work and family life, especially with your aging parents to take care of?
It can be challenging, but I try to prioritize my time and make sure I'm spending quality time with my loved ones. I also make sure to take breaks and practice origami to relax.
Origami can be very therapeutic! It's great that you're finding ways to manage your stress. What's the most challenging part of taking care of your aging parents?

I think it's the emotional toll it can take. It's hard to see them struggle with their health and not be able to do the things they used to do. But I'm trying to stay positive and focus on the present moment.
It's wonderful that you're being so supportive and caring. Remember to take care of yourself too, both physically and emotionally.
Thanks for the advice. I'm actually planning to take a trip to the Azores next month to visit my sister and get some rest. I'm looking forward to it.
The Azores are beautiful! You should definitely take some time to relax and enjoy the scenery. What are you most looking forward to doing during your trip?
I'm looking forward to trying some of the local cuisine and taking a few scenic hikes. I've heard the trails are stunning.
That sounds like a great plan! Don't forget to take care of your health while you're traveling. Make sure to stay hydrated and get enough rest.
Will do, thanks for the reminder. I'm also planning to bring my chess set and play a few games with my sister.
That's a great idea! It's always nice to have some friendly competition. Have a safe trip to the Azores and enjoy your time with your sister.
